author: Casey

title: Complete guide to tree traversal for the midterm
body: Tree traversal methods are critical for this course. Pre-order visits root before children, in-order gives sorted output for a BST, and post-order is used for deletion and expression tree evaluation. BFS uses a queue and processes nodes level-by-level, great for shortest paths. For the midterm I would recommend being able to manually walk through any of these on a 6-8 node tree, and know that all traversals run in O(n) time since every node is visited exactly once. Also review how recursion translates to an explicit stack for iterative DFS, that pattern tends to show up on exams.
author: Alex

title: Carnot cycle vs real engines - confused on efficiency

title: Midterm 2 study group, anyone else finding it tough?
